summary.intent: Build a React Native Expo web app with an Express/WebSocket API server for the Gossamer project to display Claude Code sessions from SQLite in real-time, then iteratively polish the UI with session linking, collapsible tool events, a tree view, and visual styling improvements.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the backend API server and Expo web app with live WebSocket updates, bidirectional plan→implementation session linking, scrollable event detail views with collapsed tool groups, a session tree screen, and multiple rounds of UI polish including markdown rendering and card-style important events.

repo: When plan mode is approved, Claude Code fires SessionEnd for the plan session and SessionStart for the new implementation session but never fires UserPromptSubmit for the approval message — permanent gap that cannot be hooked

Plan→Implementation session linking heuristic: SessionStart within 30s of SessionEnd with matching cwd sets parentSessionId via state.agentParents, the same mechanism already used for subagent linking
childSessionIds for bidirectional links is derived at query time from parentSessionId — no schema changes needed
NodeNext module resolution requires .js extensions on all local imports in src/server.ts
Dynamic import of server.ts in the serve subcommand prevents express/ws from loading on every hook invocation
WebSocketServer shares port 3000 with Express via WebSocketServer({ server: httpServer }) — no separate WS port needed

code.finding: session?.childSessionIds.map() crashes when childSessionIds is undefined — optional chaining only guards session being null, not the nested property; must use session?.childSessionIds?.map()

Two-pass groupEvents(): first pass pairs PreToolUse with matching PostToolUse by tool_use_id into merged toolUse items; second pass collapses consecutive toolUse + Notification items into a single toolGroup

@react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs; flex:1 resolves to 0 height on web so ScrollView/FlatList never clip. Fix: useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() as explicit height + overflow:'scroll' on a plain View
COL_WIDTHS constants must be exported from SessionRow and imported by the header row in ActiveSessions — any other approach causes column misalignment between header and data rows
Moving the left border and background to the wrapper View (not just the header row) eliminates the white sliver gap between the collapsed header and its expanded children

workflow: react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm; correct version for Expo 52 is 18.3.1
@expo/metro-runtime is required for Expo web but is not included by default — must be installed separately via npx expo install
API server must be restarted after a build to pick up response type changes; a stale process causes type mismatch crashes in the frontend (field present in code, missing in response)
npx expo install <package> resolves the correct peer version for the current Expo SDK, avoiding manual version guessing
FlatList nested inside a horizontal ScrollView fights for scroll ownership on web — replace with plain View maps wrapped in the appropriate scroll container

friction: react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm — install failed and required changing to 18.3.1
Missing @expo/metro-runtime caused Expo web to fail silently on first start

Scrolling required three failed attempts (ScrollView→FlatList, nested ScrollViews, FlatList again) before the root cause was identified as absolute-positioned screen containers from @react-navigation/stack
EADDRINUSE errors from lingering smoke-test server processes interfered with subsequent test runs
Stale API server (pre-rebuild) returned responses without childSessionIds, causing a crash that required both the optional chaining fix and a server restart to resolve
session?.childSessionIds.map() subtle JS pitfall: optional chaining guards the left side being null/undefined, not a missing property on a non-null object
open_items: SubagentStart hook events not yet captured in the DB; once they start flowing, automatic subagent linking in handler.ts will produce more accurate parent-child trees than the 30s heuristic
f6ec58ae→cd33c72b may be a false-positive link from backfilled data where updatedAt was set to last DB event time rather than actual session end — reconstruct backfill logic may need correction
No force-directed or graph layout visualization added — only an indented tree view; D3-force or a network graph library would be needed for a true graph view
MarkdownView.tsx is a hand-rolled renderer with limited coverage of markdown edge cases — a dedicated library like react-native-markdown-display would handle more cases
No automated tests for Express routes, WebSocket behavior, or React Native components
